Records.authors: Anastasiou, Charalampos, Beerli, Stefan, Bucherer, Stefan, Daleo, Alejandro, Kunszt, Zoltan
Records.description: Parent topology TP2 for two-loop $gg\to H$ via massive quark loop. All-massive box-triangle with one massless internal line $(k_1-k_2)^2$. Six massive and one massless propagator. Hosts master integrals GLASSES, TRIBUB, MP5, MP6.

Two-loop planar double-box family for $gg\to HH$ with 6 massive ($m_t$) and 1 massless internal propagator. The massless propagator is the rung $(k_1-k_2)^2$ connecting the two loops. Master integrals defined with 9 propagators but the last 2 indices (ISPs) are always zero. Expanded in small $m_t^2$ to sufficient depth for the amplitude at $\mathcal{O}(m_t^{16})$, results in HPLs with alphabet $\{-1,0\}$ in variable $t/s$ and powers of $\log(-m_t^2/s)$. Boundary conditions from method of regions + Mellin-Barnes + PSLQ. Five crossing families defined: $D_4 = D_6(p_1,p_4,p_3,p_2)$, $D_5 = D_6(p_1,p_2,p_4,p_3)$, $D_8 = D_6(p_4,p_1,p_3,p_2)$, $D_{10} = D_6(p_3,p_1,p_4,p_2)$, $D_{11} = D_6(p_3,p_1,p_2,p_4)$. Total 27 master integrals across $D_6$ and its crossings. Integration measure: $(\mu^2)^{(4-d)/2} e^{\varepsilon \gamma_E} d^d k / (i \pi^{d/2})$.

Two-loop massive double-box for light-by-light scattering. 7 propagators (6 massive, 1 massless rung) + 2 ISPs. 29 master integrals in $d=4-2\varepsilon$. Weight-4 MPLs. D-dim alphabet includes $\sqrt{16+16u+8v+v^2}$.

Two-loop four-point planar double-box of Bonciani et al. (denoted $I^A_{70}$) with six massive propagators (the two external arcs) and a single massless rung $x_7 = (k_1-k_2)^2$; same kinematics as the box-triangle (one off-shell leg $p_4$). The hepta-cut reduces to a one-fold integral over a quartic and evaluates to an algebraic (rational-with-square-root) function, demonstrating that the elliptic character of the uncut integral arises only from its sub-topologies and not from the top sector.

Third planar two-loop four-point family (called PLC in the paper), a planar double box with the outer lines of both boxes running through the massive quark and only the middle rung massless. Defined by 9 denominators; all 9 correspond to genuine graph edges in the top sector (no ISPs), with the fully massive six-edge plus rung structure carrying the heavy-quark mass. Used as one of the five families into which all two-loop amplitudes for di-jet and photon+jet production through a heavy-quark loop are reduced, with the canonical basis provided in the Zenodo ancillary. Results computed analytically as iterated integrals (including elliptic kernels) through $\mathcal{O}(\varepsilon^0)$.

Called $I_5(u,v)$ in the paper. 2-loop planar double box with massive outer propagators (common mass $m$) and one massless middle rung. 4 massless external legs, 7 propagators (6 massive, 1 massless). Finite in $d=4$, uniform transcendental weight 4. Previously computed by Caron-Huot and Henn (1404.2922), bootstrapped by Hannesdottir et al. (2410.02424). 12-letter alphabet with square roots. Rationalized via $w,z$ variables. Prefactor $P_5(u,v)=u^2 v/(m^6 \sqrt{(u+1)(u+v+1)})$. Fitted with 200 points. AMFlow: 80 CPU-min/point, 30 digits.

Two-loop planar double-box integral with massless external legs, one massless internal propagator and six equal-mass internal propagators. A scalar contribution to $\gamma \gamma \to \gamma \gamma$ and $g g \to g g$. After partial quadratization and the $x$-integration the result is written as a generalized Fox function in $s, t, u$ and $m^2$.
Records.familyName: 1-mass planar double box (6 massive + 1 massless internal)

Records.description: Two-loop planar double-box family for gg->HH with 6 massive (mt) and 1 massless internal propagator. The massless propagator is the rung (k1-k2)^2 connecting the two loops. Master integrals defined with 9 propagators but the last 2 indices (ISPs) are always zero. Expanded in small mt^2 to sufficient depth for the amplitude at O(mt^16), results in HPLs with alphabet {-1,0} in variable t/s and powers of log(-mt^2/s). Boundary conditions from method of regions + Mellin-Barnes + PSLQ. Five crossing families defined: D_4 = D_6(p1,p4,p3,p2), D_5 = D_6(p1,p2,p4,p3), D_8 = D_6(p4,p1,p3,p2), D_10 = D_6(p3,p1,p4,p2), D_11 = D_6(p3,p1,p2,p4). Total 27 master integrals across D_6 and its crossings. Integration measure: (mu^2)^{(4-d)/2} e^{eps*gamma_E} d^d k / (i pi^{d/2}).

Names: 1-mass planar double box (6 massive + 1 massless internal)
